destinations.evidence: The settlement reader omits missing jobs and inserts only existing jobs into the result map.
Tests assert missing-only reads return Ok(empty) and mixed reads return only existing jobs.

equivalence_argument: The function uses continue on missing executor and only inserts decoded SettlementData after successful existence checks, so absent job IDs are omitted from the result without failing the read.
notes: This is backed by both implementation and tests for empty/mixed missing-job cases.
rationale: The merge changes the zero-executor branch from returning an error to continue, and tests assert a missing job returns an empty result while a mixed request returns only the existing job. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: read_jobs_for_settlement must skip job ids whose jobExecutor storage is zero and return the existing jobs it can read, rather than failing the entire settlement read for a missing job.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call read_jobs_for_settlement with one existing job id and one missing job id and assert the returned map contains only the existing job and no error; call it with only a missing job and assert Ok(empty).
summary: Settlement reads omit missing jobs and return existing ones.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call read_jobs_for_settlement with one existing job id and one missing job id and assert the returned map contains only the existing job and no error; call it with only a missing job and assert Ok(empty).
title: Settlement reads omit missing jobs
